jokes.text: What the hell is that? Is that you? [ROOSTER CROWS]
jokes.setup: Mysterious annoying noise disrupting sleep
jokes.punchline: One roommate suspects the other is making rooster sounds
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's chick-becomes-rooster premise, solid confusion comedy

jokes.text: Boy, you're really not a morning person. - Just back off!
jokes.setup: Reasonable observation about being grumpy
jokes.punchline: Extreme hostile overreaction proves the point
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement/overreaction dynamic, well-executed character beat

jokes.text: It's the chick. She's going through some changes.
jokes.setup: Euphemistic explanation for strange behavior
jokes.punchline: Treating gender transition as casual 'changes'
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated delivery of absurd situation, very quotable

jokes.text: Well, the vet seems to think that she's becoming a rooster. We're getting a second opinion.
jokes.setup: Seeking professional medical advice
jokes.punchline: Treating impossible biological transformation as debatable diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist premise treated with perfect deadpan logic, highly quotable

jokes.text: Well, I've been up since 6... thanks to somebody's dumb-ass rooster.
jokes.setup: Question about early shopping
jokes.punchline: Blaming the gender-confused chick for sleep disruption
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that maintains the running gag

jokes.text: Yeah, especially not with all these knives and cookbooks around.
jokes.setup: Animals shouldn't be in apartments
jokes.punchline: Implying Monica might cook the pets
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's perfectly calibrated to Monica's chef character, economical and sharp

jokes.text: see if I'm ready... to have Frank and Alice's embryo transferred into my uterus.
jokes.setup: Going to fertility doctor
jokes.punchline: Casually dropping incredibly personal medical information
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe oversharing, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Oh, I can check that for you.
jokes.setup: Discussion of endometrial thickness
jokes.punchline: Joey offering to personally examine Phoebe's uterine lining
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey inappropriate helpfulness, maximum impact with minimal words

jokes.text: She's mad because I know today's her laundry day... and that means she's wearing her old-lady underpants.
jokes.setup: Rachel is angry about something
jokes.punchline: Revealing intimate knowledge of underwear rotation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the knowledge contest premise, uncomfortably specific

jokes.text: I can check that for you.
jokes.setup: Joey already offered to check something medical
jokes.punchline: Repeating the same inappropriate offer
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that establishes Joey's running gag for the episode

jokes.text: You can only eat Tic Tacs in even numbers.
jokes.setup: Proving they know the girls better
jokes.punchline: Revealing Monica's bizarre OCD habit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica quirk that's specific and believable, very quotable

jokes.text: You're good. These are not.
jokes.setup: Chandler correctly guesses cookies in bag
jokes.punchline: Admitting his accuracy while insulting the cookies
jokes.explanation: Nice quick beat that combines acknowledgment with deflection

jokes.text: Challenge extended. - Deal. - Challenge accepted.
jokes.setup: Formal challenge being issued
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic formal acceptance language
jokes.explanation: Sets up the competitive premise with mock formality

jokes.text: All right, we'll start with... apples. We'll be starting with apples.
jokes.setup: Joey about to guess first item
jokes.punchline: Chandler forcibly stopping and correcting him
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Chandler's control-freak side

jokes.text: I got it. Scotch... tape.
jokes.setup: Joey pausing dramatically before final guess
jokes.punchline: Mundane office supply after dramatic buildup
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection with dramatic pause building to anticlimactic answer

jokes.text: Well, we used up theirs last night making scary faces.
jokes.setup: How did you know she'd buy tape?
jokes.punchline: They wasted the roommates' tape on childish activity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd and specific detail that reveals character immaturity, highly quotable

jokes.text: Cough it up. Pay the piper.
jokes.setup: Demanding payment of bet
jokes.punchline: Joey mixing metaphors and clichés
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey malapropism, in character but not exceptional

jokes.text: It's not like he has a job or a child or a life of his own.
jokes.setup: Ross volunteered to make up questions
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically listing his many responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's put-upon feeling, well-delivered sarcasm

jokes.text: Oh, I knew it. I knew it. I felt really thick this morning.
jokes.setup: Doctor says uterus is ready for implantation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe celebrating feeling 'thick' in inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe misunderstanding medical terminology in endearing way

jokes.text: Five? Okay, where am I giving birth... in a hospital or a big box under the stairs?
jokes.setup: Learning she'll get five embryos
jokes.punchline: Imagining giving birth like a dog in hiding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation that's perfectly Phoebe, very quotable

jokes.text: I was thinking, what are the odds... if, like, you stuff, like, 200 of them in there?
jokes.setup: Low odds of pregnancy with five embryos
jokes.punchline: Suggesting jamming 200 embryos in for better odds
jokes.explanation: Good Frank Jr. cluelessness about biology, appropriately crude

jokes.text: Sweetie, now... she's a woman, not a gumball machine.
jokes.setup: Frank's crude suggestion about stuffing embryos
jokes.punchline: Comparing reproductive system to candy dispenser
jokes.explanation: Exceptional metaphor that's both absurd and perfectly corrective, extremely quotable

jokes.text: That's a lot of pressure on me and my uterus.
jokes.setup: Learning this is their only chance due to cost
jokes.punchline: Including her uterus as a separate entity feeling pressure
jokes.explanation: Clever personification of body part that's quintessentially Phoebe

jokes.text: Why doesn't she get drunk? That worked for a bunch of girls in my high school.
jokes.setup: Looking for ways to ensure pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Suggesting alcohol based on teen pregnancy experience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that fits Frank's character but borders on inappropriate

jokes.text: Animals dressed as humans.
jokes.setup: What is Monica's biggest pet peeve?
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and absurd phobia
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific Monica quirk that's absurd yet believable, very quotable

jokes.text: Michael Flatley, Lord of the Dance.
jokes.setup: What phenomenon scares Chandler?
jokes.punchline: Specific Irish dance performer
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific fear that's random enough to be funny

jokes.text: His legs flail about as if independent from his body.
jokes.setup: Why does Michael Flatley scare Chandler?
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of uncontrolled leg movement
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual description that perfectly captures Chandler's neurotic observation style

jokes.text: Althea? What are you doing? - Took a shot. Shooting with Althea?
jokes.setup: Chandler guesses grandmother's name is Althea
jokes.punchline: Joey making shooting pun on the name
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that's more groan-worthy than funny, but fits Joey's character

jokes.text: I'm afraid the TV Guide comes to 'Chanandler Bong.'
jokes.setup: What name is on Chandler and Joey's TV Guide?
jokes.punchline: Completely mangled version of Chandler's name
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment that became cultural reference, perfect absurd name mangling

jokes.text: Actually, it's Miss Chanandler Bong.
jokes.setup: Ross reveals the mangled name
jokes.punchline: Chandler correcting the title to make it even more absurd
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes the absurd name even funnier with gender specification

jokes.text: I'm hoping to be your uterus for the next nine months.
jokes.setup: Phoebe talking to embryos
jokes.punchline: Introducing herself as a temporary uterus
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Phoebe moment that treats surrogacy with characteristic directness

jokes.text: Oh, and also, next time you see me, if I'm screaming, don't worry. That's what's supposed to happen.
jokes.setup: Phoebe reassuring embryos
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning the screaming pain of childbirth
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about childbirth delivered with Phoebe's characteristic matter-of-factness

jokes.text: Viva Las Gaygas.
jokes.setup: Name of Chandler's father's Las Vegas burlesque show
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'Viva Las Vegas' with 'gay'
jokes.explanation: Clever pun that fits the show's context, memorable and quotable

jokes.text: I majored in Lightning Rounds, all right?
jokes.setup: Monica claiming to be good at lightning rounds
jokes.punchline: Treating game show format as academic subject
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitive overstatement, treating trivial skill as credential

jokes.text: Oh, I'm so confused as to what we've been doing so far.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if they want to bet
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically noting they've been betting all along
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation delivered with perfect Ross tone

jokes.text: How about if we win, they have to get rid of the rooster.
jokes.setup: Looking for stakes other than money
jokes.punchline: Bringing back the rooster problem from opening
jokes.explanation: Good callback that raises stakes and connects to episode's opening

jokes.text: But if we win... we get your apartment.
jokes.setup: Negotiating stakes for the bet
jokes.punchline: Dramatically escalating to taking their home
jokes.explanation: Major plot escalation that raises stakes dramatically, well-structured reveal

jokes.text: Look at my hand. Why, do you have the answers written on there? No. Steady as a rock.
jokes.setup: Monica showing confidence
jokes.punchline: Rachel's suspicious question followed by Monica's oblivious confidence
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay showing Monica's overconfidence and Rachel's doubt

jokes.text: Big Fat Goalie.
jokes.setup: Monica's field hockey nickname question
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest cruel nickname
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor that reveals Monica's past weight issues

jokes.text: Weekend at Bernie's.
jokes.setup: Rachel's actual favorite movie vs. claimed favorite
jokes.punchline: Lowbrow comedy instead of sophisticated choice
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation about Rachel's secret taste for lowbrow entertainment

jokes.text: [Monica's shocked reaction to pencil location]
jokes.setup: Question about where Monica got pencil stuck
jokes.punchline: Monica's horrified realization of what they're implying
jokes.explanation: Visual reaction beat to implied inappropriate pencil location

jokes.text: Okay, everyday use. - Fancy. - Guest. - Fancy guest. - Two seconds. - Uh, eleven?
jokes.setup: How many towel categories does Monica have?
jokes.punchline: Rapidly listing increasingly specific categories before wild guess
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica OCD joke with great escalating structure and timing

jokes.text: Fourteen? - No, 19. Thanks, man.
jokes.setup: When did Chandler first touch a girl's breast?
jokes.punchline: Embarrassingly late age revealed by his friend
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about Chandler's late development, his sarcastic thanks adds to it

jokes.text: Oh! Oh! He's a transponster. That's not even a word!
jokes.setup: Pressure to name Chandler's job under time constraint
jokes.punchline: Made-up word followed by immediate self-correction
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment that became cultural reference, perfect panic-induced word creation

jokes.text: Sure I can. Standard Shotgun Rules. I'm in sight of the room and I called it.
jokes.setup: Chandler objects to Joey calling Monica's room
jokes.punchline: Applying car seat rules to apartment living
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic applying inappropriate rules to new situation

jokes.text: Bet you can't guess what color my tonsils are. I bet the apartment.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming to feel sick
jokes.punchline: Trying to bet apartment on impossible-to-guess detail
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist attempt to reverse their fortune through impossible bet

jokes.text: I mean, they are, like, literally putting all their eggs in my basket.
jokes.setup: Phoebe worried about the pressure of surrogacy
jokes.punchline: Literal application of idiom to actual situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literal interpretation of idiom that perfectly fits the actual situation

jokes.text: Well, the doctor says it takes a couple days. But, I mean, my body's always been a little faster than Western medicine, so...
jokes.setup: Can you tell if you're pregnant this soon?
jokes.punchline: Phoebe claiming her body outperforms medical science
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe mystical confidence about her body's superiority to science

jokes.text: You bet on a bet, and if you lose, you lose the bet.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she won't honor the bet
jokes.punchline: Joey's tautological explanation of betting
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey circular reasoning that's endearing in its simplicity

jokes.text: Chanandler Bong. Come on, we steal that TV Guide every week.
jokes.setup: Monica blaming Rachel for losing
jokes.punchline: Revealing they've been stealing the TV Guide all along
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds another layer to the earlier joke with theft revelation

jokes.text: Yeah, good. I'm gonna let gravity, you know, do its jobs.
jokes.setup: Phoebe lying down to help with pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Pluralizing 'job' incorrectly while discussing gravity
jokes.explanation: Minor Phoebe malapropism that fits character but isn't particularly memorable

jokes.text: This is a girl's apartment. That is a boy's apartment. It's dirty and it smells.
jokes.setup: Rachel refusing to move
jokes.punchline: Contrasting clean feminine space with gross masculine one
jokes.explanation: Standard gender stereotype humor that's functional but predictable

jokes.text: And now I have got the steady hand.
jokes.setup: Rachel getting increasingly agitated
jokes.punchline: Claiming the steady hand while clearly losing composure
jokes.explanation: Good ironic callback showing how the tables have turned

jokes.text: How'd it go? - I lost our mattresses.
jokes.setup: Joey went to handle something
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he lost their beds too
jokes.explanation: Great offscreen escalation delivered with perfect casual timing

jokes.text: Are you in there, little fetus? In nine months, will you come greet us? I will buy you some Adidas
jokes.setup: Phoebe singing to potential fetus
jokes.punchline: Rhyming 'greet us' with brand name 'Adidas'
jokes.explanation: Sweet Phoebe moment with forced rhyme that's endearingly awkward

jokes.text: Hey, don't mix those up. You could really ruin that lollipop.
jokes.setup: Giving Phoebe pregnancy test and lollipop
jokes.punchline: Warning about contaminating candy rather than test accuracy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect priorities inversion, very quotable

jokes.text: So, um, you feel like taking a test? There's only one question.
jokes.setup: Asking if Phoebe wants to take pregnancy test
jokes.punchline: Treating binary pregnant/not pregnant as simple quiz
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that lightens tense moment with test format humor

jokes.text: You know what? You are mean boys who are just being mean.
jokes.setup: Rachel losing argument about apartment
jokes.punchline: Regressing to childish name-calling
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's frustration through regression to playground insults

jokes.text: My sister's going to have my baby!
jokes.setup: Celebrating positive pregnancy test
jokes.punchline: Phrasing that sounds incestuous without context
jokes.explanation: Good accidental double entendre that creates momentary confusion

jokes.text: [Both screaming at something in drawer]
jokes.setup: Monica looking for garbage bags in drawer
jokes.punchline: Both women screaming at mysterious drawer contents
jokes.explanation: Standard horror reaction to gross bachelor pad discovery

jokes.text: But maybe if we keep that drawer shut, it'll die.
jokes.setup: Something horrifying in the drawer
jokes.punchline: Suggesting containment will kill whatever's living in there
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about bachelor pad conditions, economical and quotable

jokes.text: Did you see the size of the closets? I can't believe we live here.
jokes.setup: Joey yelling in apparent distress
jokes.punchline: He's actually excited about the large closets
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where apparent distress is actually delight
